blurb: A user can turn on Qlippy (two switches deep) as a dock sprite that mirrors the runtime and slides out a card only for an action awaiting approval, a correction that reached past dictations, or a meeting that ended with open items; approving sends the identical audited request.
web: n/a — native desktop presence surface, not the web page
ipad: no — desktop-only
hints: Enable Qlippy under Settings > Desktop presence, trigger a proposal: expect a decision card with the exact preview + egress badge
Approve on his card: expect the identical audited request the dashboard sends
Dismiss him: expect it is always safe, no action taken
expand: Native overlay (macOS + Linux, Phase 41/56); off by default. Learned-from-you card only appears with a real count. Panel takes pointer clicks only while a card shows, never steals keyboard focus.

title: Profile shapes sync across your surfaces so the same named profile is available everywhere
blurb: A user can author a profile once and have its shape sync across desktop hub, iPad, iPhone, and web, with each surface holding its own key and a surface honestly showing a kind it cannot host as unavailable.
web: yes — MODELS.md 'The shape syncs across your surfaces'; GET /api/sync/pull + POST /api/sync/push
ipad: yes — ios consumes /api/sync/pull + push; MODELS.md iPad Keychain holds the key
iphone: yes — MODELS.md iPhone Keychain; same sync path
hints: Author a profile on the hub, pull sync on iPad and iPhone: expect the same named profile everywhere
On the web (browser) surface, view an on-device GGUF profile: expect it shown unavailable
expand: Parity + secrets claim combined at the sync seam. Key never syncs (see trust.secrets row).
